name: Layout & Structure
description: How the page structure and visual hierarchy should support natural scanning and discovery of key product information

heuristics.principle: The page should follow a clear vertical flow that supports natural scanning
checkpoints: Content follows a logical top-to-bottom reading flow
No horizontal scrolling required to view key content
Content width is appropriate for comfortable reading

heuristics.principle: Critical information such as price, delivery, returns and key specs should not be hidden in horizontal tabs
checkpoints: Price is visible without switching tabs
Delivery information is accessible without tab interaction
Returns policy is visible or clearly linked
Key specifications are not buried in tab interfaces

heuristics.principle: Expandable sections should be clearly labelled and easy to open without losing context
checkpoints: Accordion or expandable sections have clear titles
Opening a section does not cause jarring page jumps
Collapsed state communicates what content is hidden

heuristics.principle: Visual hierarchy should prioritise product name, imagery, price and the primary action
checkpoints: Product imagery dominates the visual hierarchy
Price is visually prominent and easy to locate
Primary CTA (Add to Cart) stands out from other elements
